metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_5527
metadata.comment:
Rationale: AMOT-130 stabilizes the actin cytoskeleton in dendritic spines as shown by FRAP assay (Fig. 4). Mutations in AMOT (e.g. AMOT lacking the C-terminus) do not stabilize. Knock-down of AMOT with two different siRNAs destabilize PSD scaffolds and triggers spine elongation (Fig. 3). RNAi-resistent AMOT rescues. The process is regulated by phosphorylation of AMOT at S175 by Lats1 kinase, which removes AMOT from the synapse or prevents its entry.. Experimental description: "Dissociated cultures of primary hippocampal neurons were prepared from E18 Sprague-Dawley rat embryos..." (p. 726)
A commercial antibody (C-18) was tested for its specificity to detect AMOT, but not the related gene products AMOT-L1 or AMOT-L2 in transfected HEK293T cells (Fig. 1 A,B).
Another antibody (#2095) generated against the N-Terminus recognizes specifically AMOT-130 and not the AMOT-80 splice isoform.
Reviewed UniProt-Accs are only available for Human (Q4VCS5) and Mouse (Q8VHG2).
